# Alexander Freeport

First mentioned: **3302-02-24**  
Last mentioned: **3302-05-20**

## Biography

Alexander Freeport formed part of the cluster of stations crippled by the technological plague during the first quarter of 3302. Alongside Ackerman Market, von Bellingshausen Port and Haack Gateway, the starport experienced the cascading systems failures that would soon be recognised as the signature of unknown artefact proximity. The station's location in the busy Eravate system made its sudden malfunction particularly disruptive for newly licensed pilots who had relied on it as a safe harbour.

Though less celebrated than Ackerman Market — which later became the focus of a cooperative meta-alloy restoration effort — Alexander Freeport shared the same fate of suspended services and uncertain recovery. Its inclusion in the affected cluster helped researchers establish the geographical pattern of the plague, demonstrating that the phenomenon was not random but followed the distribution routes of trade vessels inadvertently carrying alien objects through major hub systems.
